title: antonina debilis
intro: Antonina debilis, also known as Cassia absus, is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions. It belongs to the Fabaceae family and is commonly recognized by its bi-colored flowers and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Antonina debilis include infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be used to treat various health issues, and further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
